ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for tuna nuggets; A0FKP (Fish products and preparations) is the closest defensible parent category anchor. Used closest_parent match type as no exact FoodEx2 code for breaded fried tuna nuggets exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tuna nuggets as a prepared dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of tuna nuggets is approximately 5 pieces (100 g), typically consumed as a main protein dish at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: Tuna 5Pc Nuggets — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tuna 5Pc Nuggets (cr_id G00010424). All core macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat and sugar are not reported in the HPB record and have been estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded fried fish nuggets (USDA SR Legacy #15264 and related entries) as the closest approved analogous source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Breaded and Fried Fish Products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for breaded fish and mixed protein-carbohydrate fried foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tuna nuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous breaded fried fish products and mixed protein-carbohydrate fried foods in the University of Sydney GI Database. The high protein content (16 g/100 g) and moderate fat (9.26 g/100 g) slow gastric emptying and blunt the glycaemic response of the breadcrumb coating. Glycaemic load per 100 g serving = GI × carbohydrate_g / 100 = 45 × 9.41 / 100 = 4.2 (low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 402 mg/100 g with insufficient potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 402 mg/100 g approaches the upper threshold for a single dish component; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 50–60 g, avoid sodium-rich dipping sauces, and pair with high-potassium vegetables to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate from breadcrumb coating with high protein blunting glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Carbohydrate is modest at 9.41 g/100 g and the high protein content (16 g/100 g) slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose rise; however, individuals with type 2 diabetes should monitor portion size, avoid high-sugar dipping sauces, and account for the refined carbohydrate contribution of the breadcrumb coating in their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying adds saturated fat and likely trace trans fat; sodium is elevated
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Deep-frying introduces saturated fat (~1.8 g/100 g) and trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), both of which are atherogenic; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption, prefer baked or grilled tuna preparations to preserve the cardioprotective omega-3 benefit of tuna, and monitor cumulative sodium intake given the 402 mg/100 g sodium content.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs and likely trace trans fat, negating tuna's omega-3 anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation and trace trans fat is pro-inflammatory; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried tuna preparations and instead choose baked or grilled tuna to benefit from its omega-3 fatty acid content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tuna Nuggets (5-piece) is a deep-fried, breaded fish dish made from tuna mince or chunks coated in seasoned breadcrumbs and fried in vegetable oil. The exterior is golden and crispy while the interior is moist and flaky with a mild savoury tuna flavour. It is a popular Western-style fast food item sold in Singapore, commonly served as a protein-rich snack or main dish at lunch or dinner. Per 100 g, it provides 187 kcal, 16 g protein, 9.26 g fat, and 9.41 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 402 mg. The high protein content makes it relatively satiating and moderately suitable for blood glucose management, though the breadcrumb coating adds refined carbohydrates and the deep-frying method introduces saturated fat and likely trace trans fat. Sodium is elevated at 402 mg/100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The deep-frying process also negates the anti-inflammatory omega-3 benefit that tuna would otherwise provide, making it unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. Best consumed in moderation with low-sodium accompaniments and without high-sugar or high-sodium dipping sauces.
common_user_queries: How many calories in tuna nuggets?
Is tuna nuggets high in sodium?
Is tuna nuggets good for weight loss?
Tuna nuggets vs fish nuggets calories
Can I eat tuna nuggets if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010424, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous breaded fried fish entries and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried fish products.
notes: Saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (3.2 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (3.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and sugar (0.8 g/100 g) are not reported in the HPB source record and have been estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for breaded fried fish products (closest approved analogous source). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; confirmed zero per the HPB source record for this preparation. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed vitamin and trace mineral data for this specific tuna nugget preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for tuna nuggets exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Halal status assumed based on typical commercial tuna nugget formulations in Singapore; kosher status not confirmed. The input record listed portions as an empty array; a standard 100 g (5-piece) serving has been assigned based on the product name and typical Singapore fast food portion norms. FoodEx2 match type corrected to 'closest_parent' (from 'broader') to conform to the allowed enumeration values: closest_parent, exact, none.
